headline: FELDA settlers' land titles delayed 30 years despite RM1.2 billion allocation
context: Over 47,000 FELDA settlers are still waiting for individual land titles despite RM1.2 billion allocated for the land titling programme since the 1990s.

cards.big: 47,000 FELDA families waited 30 years for land titles — RM1.2 billion spent, still waiting
cards.sub: Second-generation settlers now inherit their parents' bureaucratic limbo

cards.big: National Land Code Section 76 requires state authority consent for title issuance
cards.sub: Federal-state jurisdiction split creates administrative gridlock for FELDA land

cards.big: Without titles, settlers cannot use land as collateral — locked out of RM4.8 billion in credit
cards.sub: Untitled land means settlers remain dependent on FELDA for financing

cards.big: Land titling task force has been restructured 4 times under different ministers
cards.sub: Institutional discontinuity means each new minister restarts the process

cards.big: FELDA settlers built Malaysia's palm oil wealth but can't prove they own the land they farm
Should FELDA settlers receive automatic title vesting after 20 years of continuous occupation?
